course_description.overview: Race Around Rwanda is a self-supported bikepacking circumnavigation of Rwanda starting and finishing at Tugende bike cafe in central Kigali. The 1000 km route features 19000 meters of elevation gain across 45% off-road terrain, earning it the nickname 'Land of a Thousand Hills.' Riders have 163 hours to complete the course with 4 mandatory checkpoints along the route.

key_segments.name: Checkpoint 1
key_segments.description: First quarter of the circumnavigation with mixed terrain

course_description.difficulty_narrative: This is a challenging ultra-distance bikepacking event combining significant elevation gain with self-supported logistics. The 45% off-road component adds technical difficulty, while Rwanda's mountainous terrain ensures relentless climbing. The 163-hour time limit (approximately 6.8 days) requires sustained pace and efficient self-management of nutrition, hydration, and bike maintenance.

logistics.getting_there: Race start and finish is at Tugende bike cafe in central Kigali, approximately 20 minutes by taxi from Rwanda's only international airport. All countries can receive visa on entry to Rwanda; African Union, Commonwealth, and Francophonie members receive free visas, while others can pay in USD or by credit card at arrival.
logistics.accommodation: This is a self-supported event with no organized accommodation. Riders must carry their own gear or utilize commercial services (hotels, restaurants) along the route. Luggage storage is provided in Kigali at the start/finish location for extra bags and bike boxes.
logistics.registration_details: Rider registration and briefing occurred on January 31, 2026. Race entry includes: route GPX file, live GPS tracking, athlete musette with t-shirt and cap, Rwandan SIM card with 7 days of data, gifts at checkpoints, finisher gift, pre-race breakfast at 3:30 AM, access to race photos, closing party, and support staff at checkpoints with on-call medical team.
logistics.bike_logistics: This is a self-supported event where riders carry all necessary equipment. No luggage transport to checkpoints is provided. Riders can purchase supplies in shops along the route and utilize local bike mechanics. A GPS tracker is provided for real-time live tracking visible at racearoundrwanda.com/live.
logistics.local_considerations: Participants must be at least 18 years old, sign a fitness declaration, and provide proof of insurance. Medical insurance covering this type of event and country is mandatory. A local SIM card with 4G functionality works on 99% of the route. The event is organized by RaR Events Ltd., a Rwanda-registered organization run by British and Belgian residents.

final_verdict.one_liner: An epic self-supported bikepacking circumnavigation through Rwanda's dramatic terrain that demands serious fitness, self-reliance, and adventure spirit.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced bikepacking racers seeking a genuine self-supported ultra-distance challenge in an exotic location. Ideal for riders comfortable with minimal support, technical off-road sections, and sustained climbing. Not suitable for first-time ultra-distance racers or those requiring extensive logistical support.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the right rider. Race Around Rwanda offers authentic adventure, stunning scenery, and a well-organized event in a unique African setting. The self-supported format and technical terrain provide genuine challenge. However, the relatively small field size and emerging prestige mean it's best suited for adventure-focused cyclists rather than those seeking competitive depth or major accolades.
pro_tips: Arrive in Kigali several days early to acclimate, test your bike setup, and scout the start area near Tugende bike cafe
Maximize the provided Rwandan SIM card with 4G data for navigation and emergency communication throughout the race
Pre-position supplies at checkpoints if possible, or identify shops near each checkpoint to resupply efficiently
Practice your self-supported setup extensively before the race; mechanical issues cannot be delegated to support crews
